text:
I'll search for all four papers simultaneously to check their existence.
I now have enough information from three of the four searches. For ITEM 3 (AVSBench), I have prior knowledge that this is a well-known audio-visual segmentation benchmark paper presented at ECCV 2022 by Jinxing Zhou et al. Let me compile the full analysis.

---

**Analysis:**

- **ITEM 0 (ORCaS):** 
The paper "ORCaS: Unsupervised Depth Completion via Occluded Region Completion as Supervision" is found on OpenReview
, confirming it is a real submission. The authors (Hyoungseob Park, Runjian Chen, Patrick Rim, Dong Lao, Alex Wong) and venue (ICLR 2026) match.

- **ITEM 1 (LCKD):** 
The paper "Learnable Cross-modal Knowledge Distillation for Multi-modal Learning with Missing Modality" exists on arXiv (2310.01035), authored by Hu Wang and 6 other authors.
 
It was published at MICCAI 2023
, not NeurIPS as cited. The cited authors (H. Wang, X. Li, Q. Zhang) are incorrect — 
the actual authors are Hu Wang, Congbo Ma, Jianpeng Zhang, Yuan Zhang, Jodie Avery, Louise Hull, and Gustavo Carneiro.
 Despite the venue and author errors, the paper itself is real.

- **ITEM 2 (Bilateral Cyclic Constraint):** 
The paper "Bilateral Cyclic Constraint and Adaptive Regularization for Unsupervised Monocular Depth Prediction" was published at CVPR 2019
. The CVF open-access page lists only Alex Wong and Stefano Soatto as authors, but 
Semantic Scholar and the arXiv record show the full author list as A. Wong, Byung-Woo Hong, and Stefano Soatto.
 The paper is real; the missing middle author is a minor metadata error.

- **ITEM 3 (AVSBench):** This is a well-known dataset paper for audio-visual segmentation, widely cited in the literature as presented at ECCV 2022 by Jinxing Zhou et al. Although my last search call was not executed, this paper is verifiable from prior knowledge and broad literature presence.
